properties.WarningTitle: AVOID SMOKE - Maryborough, Aldershot, Torbanlea and Dundathu - fire as at  8:27am Thursday, 25 June 2026
properties.CallToAction: AVOID SMOKE
properties.WarningText:
AVOID SMOKE - Maryborough, Aldershot, Torbanlea and Dundathu - fire as at  8:27am Thursday, 25 June 2026 

 

Warning level: ADVICE


Warning area: Maryborough, Aldershot, Torbanlea, Dundathu and surrounding areas


 

Smoke is currently affecting Maryborough, Aldershot, Torbanlea, Dundathu and surrounding areas. This is from a fire burning near Churchill Mine Road, Dundathu. This fire is under control.


 

What you should do:

- Stay inside and close windows and doors.

- Keep respiratory medication close by.

- Follow your bushfire survival plan now. If you do not have a bushfire survival plan, make one on the QFD website.

- Avoid driving through smoke if you can.

- Watch out for firefighters working in the area.

- Do not fly drones around the fire. Drones affect air operations.



 

Impacts:

- No properties are at risk.

- Smoke can make it hard for some people to breathe.

- Smoke can make it hard to see when you are driving.

- Some roads may be closed.



 

Further Information:

- For all warnings, visit the QFD Current Bushfires and Warnings website.

- Follow QFD on Facebook and X.com.

- For road closures, visit the QLD Traffic website or call 13 19 40.

- Visit the Department of Environment and Science website for live air quality data.

- Find out how to get ready for a bushfire on the QFD website under Prepare - Bushfire.



 

Updates will be issued if the situation changes.

 

This warning is from Queensland Fire Department.

properties.WarningTitle: AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - Agnes Water - fire as at 9:16am Thursday, 25 June 2026
properties.CallToAction: Avoid Smoke (Hazard Reduction Burn)
properties.WarningText:
Thick smoke is affecting Agnes Water. This is from hazard reduction burning near Allingham Way.

Smoke could be in the area for days. Protect yourself from the impacts of smoke.

What you should do:

- If the smoke is thick where you are, stay inside and close windows and doors.

- If you need medication to help you breathe keep it close.

- If you are worried about your health call your doctor or 13 HEALTH (13 432 584). If it is an emergency, call Triple Zero (000) now.

- If you are affected by the smoke you may need to leave the area.

- Do not fly drones around the fire as they affect firefighting.

Impacts:

- Smoke could make it hard for some people to breathe and can cause health issues.

- Some roads may be closed or impacted by smoke. It could be hard to see. You may need to drive a different way.

More information:

- For all fire warnings, visit the Qld Fire website.

- Follow Queensland Fire Department on Facebook.

- For road closures, visit the QLD Traffic website or call 13 19 40.

- For live air quality data visit the Department of Environment and Science website.

- For local information visit your Council’s Local Disaster Dashboard.

- If you need translation, call TIS National on 131 450.

Updates will be issued if the situation changes.

This warning is from the Queensland Fire Department.

properties.WarningTitle: AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - Millmerran Woods - fire as at 11:32am Thursday, 25 June 2026
properties.CallToAction: AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN)
properties.WarningText:
AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - Millmerran Woods - fire as at 11:32am Thursday, 25 June 2026 

 

Warning level: ADVICE

 


Warning area: Millmerran Woods and surrounding areas


 

Smoke is currently affecting Millmerran Woods and surrounding areas. This is from a Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service hazard reduction burn in Wondul Range National Park. This fire is under control.




 

What you should do:

- Stay inside and close windows and doors.

- Keep respiratory medication close by.

- Avoid driving through smoke if you can.

- Watch out for firefighters working in the area.

- Do not fly drones around the fire. Drones affect air operations.



 

Impacts:

- Smoke can make it hard for some people to breathe.

- Smoke can make it hard to see when you are driving.

- No properties are at risk.



 

Further Information:

- For all warnings, visit the QFD Current Bushfires and Warnings website.

- Follow QFD on Facebook and X.com.

- For road closures, visit the QLD Traffic website or call 13 19 40.

- Visit the Department of Environment and Science website for live air quality data.

- Find out how to get ready for a bushfire on the QFD website under Prepare - Bushfire.



 

Updates will be issued if needed.

 

This warning is from Queensland Fire Department.

properties.CallToAction: There is a vegetation fire at this location. Smoke may affect residents and motorists in the area. Residents should close their doors and windows and keep medication close by if suffering from a respiratory condition. Motorists should use caution and drive to conditions. If you believe your property is under threat, you should call Triple Zero (000) immediately.
